name: Kuva The Sky That Takes Many Names
description:
Kuva The Sky That Takes Many Names

*{Lore Kuva is the dome above all roads --- the endless blue, the monsoon wrath, the night's silent gaze. To the Kuvani of the eastern steppe, Kuva is a stern witness who hears every promise made under open heaven. To the Dhaharan of the fertile lowlands, Kuva is a storm-mother, destroyer and renewer, whose rains demand sacrifice and give life. To the Tulkani wanderers, Kuva is a shape-changing presence --- neither kind nor cruel, only watchful, and a slighted sky brings ill fortune. To the Aelinnel, Kuva is the first counter, the one who taught the counting song. To the Aelaerem, Kuva is the Hollow's boundary --- the sky is where the Hollow's gaze stops.

Kuva has no single shrine because Kuva is the roof of every shrine. When a Kuvani pours milk upward, when a Dhaharan offers blood at a monsoon bell, when a Tulkani ties a knot and throws ash, Kuva watches. It does not correct. It remembers.

quote "Kuva does not answer in words. It answers in weather. A clear day means "go.' A storm means "wait.' Lightning means "run.' Learn to read the sky, and you will never need a priest." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Witness of Oaths: promises made under open sky are binding Storm & Weather: rain, lightning, wind, and the monsoon's rhythm Sky as Ledger: the dome records every deed, every lie, every breath Threshold of the Hollow: where the sky's gaze ends, the Hollow begins itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Kuva does not bind a creature of the shelter --- they bind a companion of the open air. Their Thiasos is a small beast or being that embodies wind and witness: a storm petrel that nests only on the highest peaks, a wind-chaser beetle that clicks its shell when a gale is coming, a gray-feathered kestrel that never lands on a roof, a silver moth that appears only during lightning storms, or a field mouse that sits perfectly still when the sky is about to break. This creature requires no food, but it must be shown the open sky each day --- a glimpse through a window is not enough. If confined for a week, it becomes Compromised (its feathers or scales grow dull, and its bearer suffers --1 die on all navigation and weather-sense rolls until it is released).

The Codex of Kuva is never a book kept under a roof. It may be a bundle of wind-knotted cords that must be untied and retied each dawn, a slate tablet that must be read in the rain, a scroll of vellum that shrivels if not exposed to the elements, or a leather pouch of stones collected from nine different hilltops. Because the sky resists being fixed in ink, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week under open sky, reciting each Rite aloud to the wind, or the words fade (treat as Compromised until re-consecrated in a storm).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Kuva may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or garment) and whisper a single word of the sky. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to a Lore rolls made outdoors, and the first time each scene the bearer would be surprised by a weather-related hazard (lightning, flash flood, sudden fog), they may reroll the resistance check (the sky warns its own). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Kuva.

rites.name: Rite of the Sky's Witness
rites.description: (Basic, 6 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A pinch of ash thrown upward, or a single breath spoken into a knot. Effect: For one scene, when you speak a promise outdoors, any listener who hears it must test Resolve (DV 3) to lie about that promise. If you break your own promise, the GM gains 2 Story Beats to spend on a sky-related complication (lightning, sudden fog, a hawk stealing your food). Push It: The oath is witnessed by a passing storm --- the liar suffers --2 dice to all rolls until they make amends under open sky. Mark 1 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: For one scene, when you speak a promise outdoors, any listener who hears it must test Resolve (DV 3) to lie about that promise. If you break your own promise, the GM gains 2 Story Beats to spend on a sky-related complication (lightning, sudden fog, a hawk stealing your food). \\

rites.name: Rite of Kuva's Gaze
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , Materials: A mirror polished under a new moon, or a piece of sky-stone (meteorite fragment). Effect: Call down a brief flash of lightning or a clap of thunder. All who see or hear it within the zone must test Spirit (DV 4) or be Stunned for one exchange. Cannot be used indoors or underground. The lightning always strikes the highest point in the zone, which may be inconvenient. Push It: The storm lingers for the scene. All ranged attacks suffer --1 die, and any fire in the zone is extinguished. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Call down a brief flash of lightning or a clap of thunder. All who see or hear it within the zone must test Spirit (DV 4) or be Stunned for one exchange. Cannot be used indoors or underground. The lightning always strikes the highest point in the zone, which may be inconvenient. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Monsoon's Due
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Zone; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A visible scar on your own flesh (cut during the rite) and a handful of earth from the place you wish to affect. Effect: Once per season, you may pray for rain (or clear skies) over a one-mile area. The weather shifts within an hour and lasts for a full day. If you pray while standing under open sky, you may also ask for a specific omen --- a double rainbow, a lightning strike on a named target, or a wind that carries a message to a distant friend. The cost is a permanent scar; Kuva will remember your debt. Push It: The weather shift is immediate and dramatic (a cloudburst or a sudden clearing), and the scar never fully heals. Mark 3 SB (Spades) as the sky's disruption draws attention. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: 1 day.
rites.effect: Once per season, you may pray for rain (or clear skies) over a one-mile area. The weather shifts within an hour and lasts for a full day. If you pray while standing under open sky, you may also ask for a specific omen --- a double rainbow, a lightning strike on a named target, or a wind that carries a message to a distant friend. The cost is a permanent scar; Kuva will remember your debt. \\
